title: Western blot (SDS-PAGE + transfer)
description: SDS-PAGE separation, wet transfer, blocking, and ECL detection steps as editable markdown.

payload.body:
## Purpose

Separate proteins by molecular weight, transfer to a membrane, and detect a target with a specific antibody.

## Materials

| Item | Working concentration |
| --- | --- |
| Precast gel (4-12% Bis-Tris) | 1 gel |
| MOPS or MES running buffer | 1X |
| Transfer buffer (Tris-glycine + 20% methanol) | 1X |
| PVDF membrane (0.45 um) | 1 sheet |
| Blocking buffer (5% nonfat milk in TBST) | 25 mL |
| Primary antibody | per datasheet, e.g. 1:1000 |
| HRP secondary antibody | 1:5000 |
| ECL substrate | 1 mL |

## Steps

1. Mix 20-30 ug lysate with 4X loading buffer + reducing agent. Heat 70 C for 10 min.
2. Load samples and 5 uL ladder. Run SDS-PAGE at 150 V for 50 min (until dye front reaches the bottom).
3. Activate PVDF in methanol 30 sec, equilibrate membrane and gel in transfer buffer 10 min.
4. Wet transfer at 100 V for 60 min at 4 C (or 30 V overnight).
5. Block in 5% milk/TBST for 1 h at room temperature with rocking.
6. Incubate primary antibody overnight at 4 C.
7. Wash 3 x 5 min in TBST. Incubate HRP secondary 1 h at room temperature.
8. Wash 3 x 5 min in TBST. Apply ECL substrate 1 min and image.

## Expected result

A single band at the predicted molecular weight. Loading-control band (e.g. GAPDH, 37 kDa) present in every lane.

## Notes and troubleshooting

- High background: increase wash time, dilute primary further, swap milk for 5% BSA.
- No signal: confirm transfer with Ponceau S, check secondary species match.

## Safety

Methanol is flammable and toxic. Acrylamide gels: handle precast only; wear nitrile gloves.
